# Three-scatter

> Scatter meshes and 3D models across any Three.js geometry. Inspired by GScatter for Blender.

`ThreeScatter` extends `THREE.Group` and places copies of one or more meshes on the surface of a `BufferGeometry` using face sampling. It supports animated/rigged models, per-seed reproducibility, collision removal, surface-normal alignment, axis-based visibility filtering, debug mode, and custom distribution weights.

## Docs

- [Guide & Introduction](./guide/index.md): Installation, basic usage, and when to use three-scatter vs MeshSurfaceSampler.
- [API Reference](./guide/api/index.md): Full constructor, Options interface, and all public methods with TypeScript signatures.

## Examples

- [Multiple Objects](./guide/examples/multi-model.md): Scatter more than one model at once.
- [Transformations](./guide/examples/transformations.md): Apply transforms to the whole group or individual objects.
- [Align to Surface](./guide/examples/align-models.md): Orient objects along face normals.
- [Seeds](./guide/examples/seeds.md): Reproducible, tweakable layouts.
- [Remove Collisions](./guide/examples/remove-collisions.md): Automatically cull overlapping objects.
- [Display by Axis](./guide/examples/display-by-axis.md): Show/hide objects by normalized axis range.
- [Take Over Mode](./guide/examples/take-over.md): Only get positions and manage your own objects.
- [Debug Mode](./guide/examples/debug-mode.md): Low-poly proxy markers for fast iteration.
- [Distribution](./guide/examples/distribution.md): Control per-model scatter percentages.
- [Model Animation](./guide/examples/model-animation.md): Scatter rigged/animated models.
- [Dispose](./guide/examples/dispose.md): Clean up geometries and materials.
